buggy_code:
import pandas as pd

# Earnings data with report_date (when earnings reported) and filing_date (when filed with SEC)
earnings = pd.read_csv('earnings.csv', parse_dates=['report_date', 'filing_date'])
prices = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', parse_dates=['date'], index_col='date')

# Merge on report_date — this is when earnings are public
# BUT: actual SEC filing (10-K, 10-Q) may come weeks later with more detail
merged = pd.merge_asof(
    prices.reset_index().sort_values('date'),
    earnings.sort_values('report_date'),
    left_on='date',
    right_on='report_date',
    direction='backward'
)

# Using EPS from report_date is fine for most purposes
# but detailed financial ratios from the 10-K filing are only available after filing_date
clean_code:
import pandas as pd

earnings = pd.read_csv('earnings.csv', parse_dates=['report_date', 'filing_date'])
prices = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', parse_dates=['date'], index_col='date')

# Use filing_date for fundamental data that requires the full SEC filing
merged = pd.merge_asof(
    prices.reset_index().sort_values('date'),
    earnings.sort_values('filing_date'),
    left_on='date',
    right_on='filing_date',
    direction='backward'
)
description: Fundamental data merged using report_date instead of filing_date for data requiring full SEC filing.
explanation: Companies report earnings (report_date) before their formal 10-K/10-Q SEC filing (filing_date). Detailed financial ratios, footnotes, and segment data are only available after the filing date, which can be 30-90 days after the earnings call. Using report_date for these detailed metrics introduces lookahead bias for the filing period.
suggested_fix: Use the appropriate date — report_date for headline EPS/revenue, filing_date for detailed financial metrics.

issues.title: Fundamental data merged on report_date before full filing is available
